int number_argument(const String &argume.brief: Extracts a leading numeric value from a String argument and retrieves the remaining non-numeric portion.
int number_argument(const String &argume.details: This function analyzes the input String 'argument' to identify and extract a leading numeric prefix. It stores the non-numeric part of the string into 'arg'. The function returns the numeric value as an integer. It internally converts the String to a C-style string for processing, then assigns the non-numeric portion back to 'arg'. This allows callers to parse strings with embedded numeric prefixes efficiently.

params.argument: The input String from which to extract the leading number.
params.arg: A String reference where the non-numeric part of 'argument' will be stored.

int number_argument(const String &argume.returns: An integer representing the numeric prefix extracted from 'argument'.
int number_argument(const String &argume.name: number_argument

Flags::Bit entity_argument(const String.brief: Parses an entity type from a string argument and updates the output string with the suffix.
Flags::Bit entity_argument(const String.details: This function takes an input argument string, extracts a numeric prefix and an entity type indicator character, and processes these to determine the corresponding Flags::Bit value. It then copies the suffix part of the input string (after the numeric prefix and entity type character) into the provided 'arg' string. The function returns the Flags::Bit value representing the parsed entity type. It acts as a wrapper around an internal implementation that operates on C-style strings, facilitating integration with the String class and managing memory accordingly.

params.argument: A String object containing the input argument, expected to include a numeric prefix followed by a character indicating the entity type.
params.arg: A String object that will be assigned the suffix part of the input argument after parsing.

Flags::Bit entity_argument(const String.returns: A Flags::Bit value representing the parsed entity type based on the input argument.
Flags::Bit entity_argument(const String.name: entity_argument

int mult_argument(const String &argument.brief: Parses a string to extract a numeric multiplier and the substring following an asterisk.
int mult_argument(const String &argument.details: This function processes the input argument, which is a String object potentially containing a numeric value followed by an asterisk and additional characters. It calls an internal overload to parse the C-style string representation of the argument, extracting the numeric multiplier and storing the substring after the asterisk into the provided String reference 'arg'. The function returns an integer indicating the parsed multiplier or a status code, depending on the internal implementation. The 'arg' parameter is updated with the substring following the asterisk if present.

params.argument: The input String object that may contain a numeric multiplier followed by an asterisk and additional characters.
params.arg: A String reference where the substring after the asterisk will be stored if found.

int mult_argument(const String &argument.returns: An integer representing the parsed numeric multiplier or a status code indicating success or failure of the parsing process.
int mult_argument(const String &argument.name: mult_argument

const char * one_argument(const String &.brief: Extracts the first argument from a string, handling quotes, and updates the provided String with this argument.
const char * one_argument(const String &.details: This function processes the input 'argument' string to extract its first argument, considering quoted segments for multi-word arguments. The extracted argument is stored in 'arg'. It returns a pointer to the position in the input string immediately following the extracted argument, allowing further parsing. The function relies on an internal helper 'one_argument' that operates on C-style strings, and it ensures the extracted argument is stored in a String object for further use.

params.argument: The input String from which the first argument will be extracted.
params.arg: A String reference where the extracted argument will be stored.

const char * one_argument(const String &.returns: A const char pointer to the position in the input string immediately after the extracted argument, useful for continuing parsing.
const char * one_argument(const String &.name: one_argument
